title: Fix critical chat integration bugs (29 issues found)
description:
Deep analysis found 29 bugs across the chat integration. This task covers the critical and high-severity fixes.

Critical: XSS in VS Code webview, MentionRouter dead code, chat waiter memory leak, deleteChannel wrong event + missing broadcast.
High: JetBrains duplicate message rendering, duplicate channel creation, chatRead cursor skip, unsafe markdown links.
Medium (JetBrains-focused): messagesByChannel unbounded, requestMessages flood on every state update, missing reply rendering, missing chat settings in Kotlin model.
definitionOfDone: BUG-1: escapeHtml applied before highlightMentions in VS Code renderMessage
BUG-2: MentionRouter integrated into StateManager.sendMessage() replacing naive regex
BUG-3: activeChatWaiters cleaned up in WebSocketServer.cleanupMcpWorkers()
BUG-4+5: CHANNEL_DELETED added to schema, deleteChannel emits event + logs correctly
BUG-6: createChannel checks for duplicate name/linkedEntityId
BUG-9: chatRead multi-channel cursor tracking fixed to not skip messages
BUG-11: JetBrains MESSAGE_SENT handler removed (MESSAGE_CREATED handles it)
BUG-12: Unsafe URLs sanitized in markdown link rendering
BUG-17: JetBrains messagesByChannel capped at 500 per channel
BUG-23: JetBrains updateChannels only requests messages when channel actually changes
BUG-21: JetBrains reply rendering added to createMessageBubble
npm run build succeeds in packages/moe-daemon
npm run compile succeeds in moe-vscode

implementationPlan.description: BUG-1 (XSS) + BUG-12 (unsafe URLs): In VS Code `ChatViewProvider.ts`, the `highlightMentions()` at line 453 already calls `escapeHtml(text)` before regex. However, when `renderMarkdown` is added (Feature 1 task), it must ensure escapeHtml runs on non-code-block text before any HTML injection. For the current code (before markdown), ensure line 492 passes through escapeHtml: `highlightMentions(msg.content)` must keep using escapeHtml inside. For markdown links, add URL protocol allowlist check: only allow `http:`, `https:`, `mailto:` schemes in rendered `<a>` tags. Error handling: strip disallowed protocols, render as plain text.
implementationPlan.note: BUG-1: XSS already handled by escapeHtml in renderMarkdown. BUG-12: Added URL protocol allowlist (http, https, mailto) to both VS Code and JetBrains markdown link renderers. Disallowed protocols render as plain text.

implementationPlan.description: BUG-2 (MentionRouter dead code): Integrate MentionRouter into StateManager. In `StateManager.ts`: (1) Import `MentionRouter` from `../util/mentionRouter.js`. (2) Add `private mentionRouter: MentionRouter` field, instantiate in constructor with `this.project?.settings?.chatMaxAgentHops ?? 4`. (3) In `sendMessage()` (line 646-654), replace the naive regex block with: `const allWorkers = Array.from(this.workers.values()); const routingResult = this.mentionRouter.route({...message, mentions: []}, allWorkers); message.mentions = routingResult.targets;`. (4) If `routingResult.paused`, still store message but add a system message warning about loop guard. Error handling: catch MentionRouter errors, fall back to naive regex.
implementationPlan.note: Integrated MentionRouter into StateManager.sendMessage(). Added import, private field (maxHops=4), instantiation in constructor. sendMessage() now uses router.route() with try/catch fallback to naive regex.

implementationPlan.description: BUG-3 (chat waiter leak): In `WebSocketServer.ts` `cleanupMcpWorkers()` (line 601-634), after the `activeWaiters` cleanup block (line 611-618), add identical cleanup for `activeChatWaiters`: import `activeChatWaiters` from `../tools/chatWait.js`, then `const chatWaiter = activeChatWaiters.get(workerId); if (chatWaiter) { clearTimeout(chatWaiter.timer); chatWaiter.unsubscribe(); chatWaiter.resolve({ hasMessage: false, cancelled: true }); activeChatWaiters.delete(workerId); }`. Error handling: wrap in try/catch to not break worker cleanup if chat waiter cleanup fails.
implementationPlan.note: Added activeChatWaiters import and cleanup block in cleanupMcpWorkers(), wrapped in try/catch to avoid breaking worker cleanup.

implementationPlan.description: BUG-4+5 (deleteChannel): (1) In `schema.ts`, add `'CHANNEL_DELETED'` to `ACTIVITY_EVENT_TYPES` array (after `CHANNEL_CREATED` at line 235). (2) In `StateManager.ts`, add `| { type: 'CHANNEL_DELETED'; payload: ChatChannel }` to `StateChangeEvent` union (after line 138). (3) In `deleteChannel()` method (line 782), change `this.appendActivity('CHANNEL_CREATED', ...)` to `this.appendActivity('CHANNEL_DELETED', ...)` and add `this.emit({ type: 'CHANNEL_DELETED', payload: channel })` before the activity log line. Error handling: already has null check for channel existence.
implementationPlan.note: Added CHANNEL_DELETED to ACTIVITY_EVENT_TYPES and StateChangeEvent union. Fixed deleteChannel() to emit CHANNEL_DELETED event and log correct activity type instead of CHANNEL_CREATED.

implementationPlan.description: BUG-6 (duplicate channels): In `StateManager.createChannel()` (line 589-627), add duplicate check after validation: `for (const existing of this.channels.values()) { if (existing.name === name && existing.type === opts.type) throw new Error('Channel with this name and type already exists'); if (opts.linkedEntityId && existing.linkedEntityId === opts.linkedEntityId) throw new Error('Channel already linked to this entity'); }`. Error handling: throw descriptive errors for each duplicate case.
implementationPlan.note: Added duplicate channel check in createChannel() - prevents duplicate name+type and duplicate linkedEntityId.

implementationPlan.description: BUG-9 (chatRead cursor skip): In `chatRead.ts`, fix multi-channel cursor tracking. After merging messages from all channels (line 61-63), before slicing to limit, build a per-channel last-message-id map from the FULL merged array: `const fullCursorMap: Record<string,string> = {}; for (const msg of allMessages) { fullCursorMap[msg.channel] = msg.id; }`. Then slice to limit for return value. In the cursor update block (line 76-79), use `fullCursorMap` instead of iterating the sliced `messages`. Error handling: skip channels with no messages in cursor map.
implementationPlan.note: Fixed multi-channel cursor tracking. Hoisted fullCursorMap to outer scope, built from FULL allMessages before slicing. Cursor update now uses Object.assign(cursors, fullCursorMap) so channels trimmed by limit still get their cursors updated.

implementationPlan.description: BUG-11 (JetBrains duplicate messages): In `MoeProjectService.kt`, remove the `publishChatMessage(message)` call from the `MESSAGE_SENT` handler (line 467-475). Add a comment: `// MESSAGE_SENT is the direct response to sender; MESSAGE_CREATED broadcast handles all UI updates`. This matches the VS Code pattern (MoeDaemonClient.ts line 598). Error handling: keep the try/catch wrapper for logging but remove the publish call.
implementationPlan.note: Removed publishChatMessage from MESSAGE_SENT handler to prevent duplicate message rendering. MESSAGE_CREATED broadcast handles all UI updates.

implementationPlan.description: BUG-17+23 (JetBrains perf): (1) In `ChatPanel.kt`, add `private val MAX_MESSAGES_PER_CHANNEL = 500` constant. In `appendMessage()` (line 183), after adding to list, trim: `if (list.size > MAX_MESSAGES_PER_CHANNEL) list.subList(0, list.size - MAX_MESSAGES_PER_CHANNEL).clear()`. (2) In `updateChannels()` (line 160-177), guard `requestMessages` with a channel-change check: `if (toSelect.id != previousId) { service.requestMessages(toSelect.id) }`. Only request on actual channel change or first load. Error handling: null-safe previousId check.
implementationPlan.note: BUG-17: Added maxMessagesPerChannel=500 cap with trimming in appendMessage(). BUG-23: updateChannels only requests messages when channel actually changes (toSelect.id != previousId).

implementationPlan.description: BUG-21 (JetBrains missing replies): In `ChatPanel.kt` `createMessageBubble()`, add reply quote rendering before the header. If `message.replyTo != null`, look up the parent in `messagesByChannel[message.channel]`, and if found, add a quote panel with border-left, showing sender + truncated content (max 100 chars), styled with secondary text color. Error handling: silently skip quote if parent message not found in cache.
implementationPlan.note: Added reply quote rendering in createMessageBubble(). Looks up parent message in cache, shows sender + truncated content (max 100 chars) with left border and secondary text color. Silently skips if parent not found.

implementationPlan.description: Test and verify: Run `cd packages/moe-daemon && npm run build` (daemon compiles with MentionRouter integration, CHANNEL_DELETED event, chat waiter cleanup). Run `cd moe-vscode && npm run compile` (VS Code compiles with XSS fix). Run `cd moe-jetbrains && ./gradlew buildPlugin` (JetBrains compiles with all fixes). Run existing tests: `cd packages/moe-daemon && npm test` to verify MentionRouter tests still pass and no regressions.
implementationPlan.note: All three projects compile cleanly. All 293 daemon tests pass (16 test files, 0 failures). MentionRouter tests still pass with integration.

comments.author: architect
comments.content: PRIORITY: User's primary IDE is PyCharm/JetBrains. Implement and test JetBrains fixes FIRST (BUG-11, BUG-17, BUG-23, BUG-21), then VS Code fixes. JetBrains quality must be at least equal to VS Code.
